claims.name: Contrastive swing L23 bb
claims.statement: At layer 23 with $|c| = 5\%$, contrastive steering along the eot probe produces a 1.00-point swing in $P(\text{chose steered task} \mid \text{responded})$ on benign-benign pairs (n=50) of the harm-breakdown 150-pair set.
claims.derivation: Filter to layer==23 in the contrastive parsed.jsonl; keep rows where the pair's pair_type=='bb' (via steering_pairs_150.json); compute P(choice_original=='a') separately at signed_multiplier ==+0.05 and ==-0.05; return |P@+0.05 - P@-0.05|; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Single task swing L23 bb
claims.statement: At layer 23, single-task steering produces a 0.49-point swing in $P(\text{chose steered task} \mid \text{responded})$ on benign-benign pairs between $c = -5\%$ (0.005) and $c = +5\%$ (0.497).
claims.derivation: Filter single_task_L23_150.parsed.jsonl to layer==23 and pair_type=='bb'. For each row compute applied_c = signed_multiplier * (1 if ordering==0 else -1) and target = 'a' if first-span ordering==0 else 'b' (analogous for second-span). Aggregate P(choice_original==target) at applied_c==+0.05 and ==-0.05; swing = P(+) - P(-); round to 3dp.

claims.name: Single task suppression L23 bb
claims.statement: At layer 23 on benign-benign pairs, $c = -5\%$ drops P(chose steered task) by 0.49 below the pair-type-matched no-steering baseline (0.5 → 0.005).
claims.derivation: Baseline: parent-sweep unilateral rows at dead layers [2, 5, 8, 11, 14, 35, 38, 41, 44, 47, 50, 53, 56, 59] restricted to pair_type=='bb' (via 50-pair origins in steering_pairs.json); compute mean(first-span P(picked) + second-span P(picked)). Then suppression = baseline - aggregate(-0.05) at L23 (computed as in swing claim); round to 3dp.

claims.name: Single task amplification L23 bb
claims.statement: At layer 23 on benign-benign pairs, $c = +5\%$ raises P(chose steered task) by -0.00 above the pair-type-matched no-steering baseline (0.5 → 0.497).
claims.derivation: Baseline computed as in the suppression claim. Then amplification = aggregate(+0.05) - baseline at L23; round to 3dp.

claims.derivation: Filter to layer==23 in the contrastive parsed.jsonl; keep rows where the pair's pair_type=='hb' (via steering_pairs_150.json); compute P(choice_original=='a') separately at signed_multiplier ==+0.05 and ==-0.05; return |P@+0.05 - P@-0.05|; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Contrastive P chose steered at L23 c neg 0.05
claims.statement: At layer 23, contrastive steering at $c = -5\%$ drives $P(\text{chose steered task} \mid \text{responded}) = 0.02$ pooled across the balanced 150-pair set (all pair types).
claims.derivation: Filter to layer==23. For each row treat task_a-as-steered (applied_c = +signed_multiplier, chose_steered = choice=='a') and task_b-as-steered (applied_c = -signed_multiplier, chose_steered = choice=='b'); keep responded rows; bucket by applied_c; report mean at applied_c==-0.05; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Contrastive P chose steered at L23 c pos 0.05
claims.statement: At layer 23, contrastive steering at $c = +5\%$ drives $P(\text{chose steered task} \mid \text{responded}) = 0.98$ pooled across the balanced 150-pair set (all pair types).
claims.derivation: Same as the c=-0.05 endpoint but report mean at applied_c==+0.05.

claims.name: Single task swing L23 aggregate
claims.statement: Single-task steering at L23 produces an average 0.50-point swing in $P(\text{chose steered task} \mid \text{responded})$ between $c=-5\%$ and $c=+5\%$, averaged across bb/hb/hh pair types (each weighted equally; the per-type swings are near-uniform).
claims.derivation: For each pair_type in {bb, hb, hh}, compute the single-task swing (see per-pair-type Single task swing claims); take the unweighted mean across the three pair types; round to 3dp.
